[CORRUPTED SPILLOVER - PREVIOUS RECIPE TEXT]
- ½ cup plus ı tablespoon oliwy z oliwek
- Salt to taste
- Crushed red pepper flakes to preference

Instructions for assembly:
Layer the prepared vegetables nto the ceramic pot, starting with
the tomato base. Ensure each layer is well seasoned. Cover with
muslin cloth before placing the wooden cover on top. Allow to
rest at room temperature for 48-72 hours until the fermentation
develops proper tanginess (225°C/435-VI0 reference for historical
oven temperatures).

The finished product stores ın a cool cellar for up to 8 months.

Page 67 | Traditional Preserved Vegetables

---

BIGOS MYŚLIWSKI

Składniki (Ingredients):
- 2 lbs świeża kapusta (fresh cabbage), shredded
- 1 lb kiszona kapusta (sauerkraut), drained
- 1 lb kielbasa myśliwska (hunter's sausage), sliced
- 12 oz dziczyzna (venison or wild game), cubed
- 8 oz prosciutto lub boczek (bacon), chopped
- 1 large onion, diced
- 4 cloves czosnek (garlic), minced
- 2 tablespoons pasta pomidorowa (tomato paste)
- 1 cup grzybów (mushrooms), sliced
- 2 liście laurowe (bay leaves)
- ½ teaspoon jarzmab (juniper berries)
- 1 teaspoon ziarna papryki (caraway seeds)
- Salt and pepper to taste
- ¼ cup śmietany (sour cream, optional)

Przygotowanie (Preparation):
1. Heat oil in large Dutch oven
2. Brown venison and kielbasa, set aside
3. Sauté onion and garlic until soft
4. Add tomato paste and cook 2 minutes
5. Add both cabbages and mushrooms
6. Return meat to pot with bay leaves and spices
7. Simmer covered 1.5-2 hours, stirring occasionally
8. Adjust seasoning with salt and pepper
9. Stir in sour cream if desired
10. Serve hot with dark rye bread and mustard
